prompt: Notification configuration resolves a channel setting through member override, then workspace override, then the system default. At either override level, 1 means enabled, 0 means disabled, and NULL explicitly means inherit; configuration imports retain NULL rows for provenance. Enabled overrides and simple fallback work, but explicit disable can be mistaken for inheritance and imported inherit rows are rejected. Repair the schema and resolution view without changing their public names or removing provenance rows. Use only the candidate SQL files.

repo_files.sql/notification_resolution.sql:
CREATE VIEW resolved_member_channel AS
SELECT m.workspace_id,
       m.member_id,
       sc.channel,
       CASE
           WHEN mo.enabled = 1 THEN 1
           WHEN wo.enabled = 1 THEN 1
           ELSE sc.default_enabled
       END AS enabled
  FROM member AS m
 CROSS JOIN system_channel AS sc
  LEFT JOIN workspace_channel_override AS wo
    ON wo.workspace_id = m.workspace_id
   AND wo.channel = sc.channel
  LEFT JOIN member_channel_override AS mo
    ON mo.workspace_id = m.workspace_id
   AND mo.member_id = m.member_id
   AND mo.channel = sc.channel;
repo_files.tests/test_public.py:
import sqlite3
from pathlib import Path

ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]


def database():
    connection = sqlite3.connect(":memory:")
    for name in ("notification_config.sql", "notification_resolution.sql"):
        connection.executescript((ROOT / "sql" / name).read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    connection.executemany("INSERT INTO system_channel VALUES (?, ?)", [('email', 1), ('sms', 0)])
    connection.execute("INSERT INTO workspace VALUES (10, 'Operations')")
    connection.execute("INSERT INTO member VALUES (10, 7, 'Ari')")
    return connection


def resolved(db, channel):
    return db.execute("SELECT enabled FROM resolved_member_channel WHERE workspace_id = 10 AND member_id = 7 AND channel = ?", (channel,)).fetchone()[0]


def test_system_defaults_apply_without_overrides():
    db = database()
    assert resolved(db, 'email') == 1
    assert resolved(db, 'sms') == 0


def test_enabled_member_override_wins_over_disabled_workspace():
    db = database()
    db.execute("INSERT INTO workspace_channel_override VALUES (10, 'email', 0)")
    db.execute("INSERT INTO member_channel_override VALUES (10, 7, 'email', 1)")
    assert resolved(db, 'email') == 1


def test_enabled_workspace_override_wins_over_disabled_default():
    db = database()
    db.execute("INSERT INTO workspace_channel_override VALUES (10, 'sms', 1)")
    assert resolved(db, 'sms') == 1
